steps: Factor the denominator of the second fraction: $x^{2}y - 10xy = xy(x-10)$. So the second fraction becomes $\frac{y(x-10)}{xy(x-10)} = \frac{1}{x}$ after canceling $(x-10)$.

The expression simplifies to $\frac{y+13}{x-10} + \frac{1}{x}$. Find a common denominator of $x(x-10)$:$$\frac{x(y+13)}{x(x-10)} + \frac{x-10}{x(x-10)} = \frac{xy+13x+x-10}{x(x-10)} = \frac{xy+14x-10}{x(x-10)}$$
Factor $y$ from numerator and denominator to match answer format: multiply numerator and denominator by $y$:$$\frac{y(xy+14x-10)}{xy(x-10)} = \frac{xy^{2}+14xy-10y}{x^{2}y-10xy}$$

Evaluate each choice:
  • A: $\frac{xy+y+3}{x^{3}y-20x^{2}y+100xy}$ — the denominator $x^{3}y - 20x^{2}y + 100xy$ corresponds to $(x-10)^2 \cdot xy$, not $x(x-10)$. Numerator also does not match. Incorrect.
  • B: $\frac{xy+11y+13}{x^{2}y-10xy+x-10}$ — the denominator mixes terms that don't factor cleanly from the original expression. Numerator doesn't match. Incorrect.
  • C: $\frac{xy^{2}+14xy-10y}{x^{2}y-10xy}$ — numerator is $y(xy+14x-10)$ and denominator is $xy(x-10)$, which equals $x^{2}y-10xy$. This exactly matches the simplified result. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: $\frac{xy^{2}+14xy-10y}{x^{3}y-20x^{2}y+100xy}$ — correct numerator but denominator $x^{3}y-20x^{2}y+100xy = xy(x-10)^{2}$ is too large. Incorrect.
